abstract.text: This article describes the KeyboardKit settings engine and its available settings.

inlineContent.text: KeyboardKit provides you with auto-persisted keyboard settings, as well as ways to integrate with System Settings. You can use this to provide users with ways to open System Settings from the app and keyboard, and to configure the keyboard extension.
The various context classes have settings properties, like
, that are used as the main settings values. The distinction between contexts and settings, is that a context provides contextual information, while settings provide persisted ones.
👑 KeyboardKit Pro unlocks various
for licenses that include the
feature. These screens can be used to configure the various library features. You can sign up for KeyboardKit Pro from the
The
type provides keyboard-related settings, and serves as a namespace for settings-related types and views.
KeyboardKit has a
type with keyboard-related settings, and feature-specific setting types like
, etc.
The
has properties like
, and
, and functions like
, and
. See the various feature articles for a list of available settings for each feature.
Each observable context type has its own settings instance. For instance
has a
. This lets you access the current settings from the current
contexts that are injected into the environment.
The various settings types persist their data in the
settings store, which automatically syncs changes between the app and its keyboard extension, if both are configured with an App Group as shown in the
and
guides.
The
namespace has general settings views, like the generic
view, and the various features have their own settings-specific screens and views, as described in each feature article.

Due to privacy concerns, Apple only allows us to access a few system settings, like being able to access the user dictionary. This is why we have to use our own keyboard settings, using the types above as well as custom app settings.
We can however open System Setting to let users configure the app and keyboard, by triggering the
URL like this:
You can also trigger the various URL-based
s to open URLs with the current
. If your app or keyboard randomly navigates to the Settings root instead of your app, try adding an empty Settings Bundle to your app.
KeyboardKit Pro unlocks settings screens for various features. These screens can be used in both the app, as well as in the keyboard.

These screens can be added to your main app, and can also be added to your keyboard extension, although the limited screen estate of a keyboard extension will make them hard to use. See each namespace for more information.
